title: pisum sativum subsp. biflorum
intro: Pisum sativum subsp. biflorum, also known as the garden pea or spring pea, is a subspecies of the Pisum sativum plant, which is a cool-season legume widely cultivated for its edible seeds. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and protect against oxidative stress. The health benefits of Pisum sativum subsp. biflorum include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve blood sugar control, and support cardiovascular health, making it a valuable addition to a balanced diet. Herbal preparations of Pisum sativum subsp. biflorum often involve using the seeds, pods, or leaves to make teas, infusions, or tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
